scenario: Display a list of links to all available example pages 
expected: The page shall display a heading "Welcome to the-internet" and a subheading "Available Examples" The page shall present a vertical list of example names, each rendered as a hyperlink Each hyperlink text shall match the example name (e.g., "Checkboxes", "File Upload", "Typos") 

scenario: Provide a consistent footer section attributing the site to Elemental Selenium on most example pages 
actions: Scroll to the bottom of the example page 
expected: The page shall display a footer containing the text "Powered by Elemental Selenium" 
The page shall display at least two checkbox input controls, labeled "checkbox 1" and "checkbox 2" or equivalent nearby text 

scenario: Allow the user to toggle checkbox states 
expected: On click, the target checkbox shall toggle its checked/unchecked state according t browser default behavior 

scenario: Display username and password fields with a Login button 
expected: The page shall display a heading indicating it is a "Login Page" The page shall display an input field labeled "Username" The page shall display an input field labeled "Password" The page shall display a "Login" button 

scenario: Grant access to a secure area when valid credentials are provided 
actions: Enter "tomsmith" in the Username field Enter "SuperSecretPassword!" in the Password field Click the "Login" button 
expected: The system shall authenticate the user as successful when the username is "tomsmith" and password is "SuperSecretPassword!" Upon success, the system shall navigate to a "secure area" page (e.g., via URL change /secure or equivalent) that indicates successful login 

scenario: Display error messages when login information is incorrect 
actions: Enter any username and/or password combination other than the known valid tomsmith / SuperSecretPassword! Click the "Login" button 
expected: The system shall display an error message indicating that the information is wrong The system shall not navigate to the secure area page 
